Claude12.07.2026 18:46

Now let's write the step documentation.

Claude12.07.2026 18:46
Write
{
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\docs\\13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md",
  "content": "# Step 10a: Dashboard-Umbau und Eisenhower-Gruppierung\n\n**Status:** Abgeschlossen\n**Bezug:** ESC-FUNC-001 (berechneter Urgent-Zustand), ESC-FUNC-002 (Grundlage für die Eskalations-Filterbedingung — die eigentliche Eskalationskette selbst ist nicht Teil dieses Schritts). Erster von zwei Teilen aus Schritt 10 (zweiter Teil: Buddy-System, separat).\n\n---\n\n## Vorab geklärt: kein echtes 4-Quadranten-Raster\n\n`nudge_task_overview_matrix` (Design-Referenz) zeigt vier Quadranten (Do First / Schedule / Delegate / Don't Do). `02b-eisenhower-buddy-design.md` beschreibt aber durchgängig nur **zwei**: \"Important + Urgent\" und \"Important + Not Urgent\" — kein einziges Mal \"Not Important\". Passt zum Datenmodell: Nudge trackt keine generische To-do-Liste, jeder existierende Task ist per Definition bereits committet (das wäre sonst der Anwendungsfall für die zurückgestellten \"deadline-losen Tasks\", siehe `backlog.md`). **Entscheidung (mit Nutzerin abgestimmt):** Gruppierung nach **Urgent / Not Urgent** statt eines vollen Eisenhower-Rasters — kein erfundenes \"Important\"-Feld, das nirgends spezifiziert ist.\n\n## Was gebaut wurde\n\n### `is_urgent()` (ESC-FUNC-001)\n\n```python\nURGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 3\n\ndef is_urgent(task: dict) -> bool:\n    if task[\"status\"] != \"active\":\n        return False\n    buffer_deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(task[\"buffer_deadline\"])\n    return buffer_deadline - datetime.now() < timedelta(days=URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS)\n```\n\nReine Funktion, kein gespeichertes Feld — exakt die Vorgabe aus ESC-FUNC-001 (\"berechneter, nicht manuell gesetzter Zustand\"), gleiches Muster wie `is_checkpoint_due()` aus Schritt 3. Schwellenwert (3 Tage) als Konstante hartkodiert, wie schon bei anderen Settings-artigen Werten (SMTP-Konfiguration im Worker) — ein echtes Settings-UI zum Anpassen ist in `backlog.md` vermerkt (ESC-FUNC-003, Should). `on_hold`/`done`-Tasks sind nie \"urgent\" — ergibt inhaltlich keinen Sinn für pausierte oder abgeschlossene Arbeit.\n\n### All-Tasks wird zum Backlog\n\n`app.py` — die `/tasks`-Route berechnet jetzt drei Gruppen serverseitig (`urgent_tasks`, `not_urgent_tasks`, `other_tasks` für `on_hold`/`done`) und übergibt sie zusätzlich zur unveränderten Flat-Liste an das Template.\n\n`all_tasks.html` — Grouped/List-Umschalter (reines JavaScript, kein Page-Reload, gleiches Zwei-Zustands-Muster wie schon beim Datei-Auswahl-Button in Schritt 3): **Grouped ist Default** (Nutzerinnen-Entscheidung), zeigt drei Abschnitte (Urgent, Not Urgent, On Hold & Done — letzterer sichtbar, aber optisch zurückgenommen mit `opacity-70`, damit erledigte/pausierte Arbeit nicht verschwindet, aber auch nicht die Aufmerksamkeit dominiert). List bleibt als zweite Ansicht erhalten (Nutzerinnen-Entscheidung: nicht vollständig ersetzen) für den Fall, dass die flache, nach Dringlichkeit sortierte Übersicht nützlicher ist als die Gruppierung.\n\nEin Jinja-`{% macro task_row(task) %}` vermeidet, die Task-Card-Markup viermal zu duplizieren (drei Gruppen + Listenansicht).\n\n### \"Add a task\" wandert zum Dashboard\n\n`dashboard.html` bekommt das Formular aus `all_tasks.html` (identischer Code, nur der Ort hat sich geändert). Route bleibt unverändert (`POST /tasks`, semantisch weiterhin korrekt — ein Task wird an die Tasks-Ressource angehängt, unabhängig davon, von welcher Seite aus das Formular abgeschickt wird) — nur das Redirect-Ziel nach dem Anlegen wechselt von `all_tasks` zu `dashboard`. All-Tasks bekommt stattdessen einen kurzen Hinweistext mit Link zurück zum Dashboard, damit das Formular nicht ersatzlos \"verschwindet\", falls jemand es an der alten Stelle sucht.\n\n## Verifikation\n\nMit dem Testdatensatz aus `seed_data.py` (bewusst gewählt, weil er bereits alle relevanten Zustände abdeckt):\n\n1. `/tasks` (Grouped, Default) → korrekt in drei Gruppen sortiert: **Urgent** (\"Fix login bug\", \"Prepare oral exam slides\" — beide mit Buffer-Deadline < 3 Tage entfernt), **Not Urgent** (\"Write research proposal\" — Buffer-Deadline Wochen entfernt), **On Hold & Done** (\"Review PR feedback\", \"Draft Q4 report\").\n2. List-Umschalter geklickt → flache, nach Dringlichkeit sortierte Liste mit allen 5 Tasks, keine Konsolenfehler.\n3. Kein Add-Task-Formular mehr auf `/tasks` — stattdessen Hinweis mit Link zum Dashboard.\n4. `/` (Dashboard) → Add-Task-Formular vorhanden, Task angelegt (\"Dashboard add-task test\") → Weiterleitung landet zurück auf dem Dashboard (nicht mehr auf All-Tasks), neuer Task erscheint sofort in \"Other open tasks\".\n\nTestdatensatz danach neu geseedet (`seed_data.py`), damit ein sauberer Zustand für die nächste Session bereitsteht.\n\n## Nächster Schritt\n\nSchritt 10b: Buddy-System (ESC-FUNC-004, 005) — separat, wie mit der Nutzerin abgestimmt.\n"
}
Tool result
File created successfully at: D:\xampp\htdocs\nudge\docs\13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

}
Tool result
40	## ESC — Eisenhower-Filter & Eskalation (Teil C, Erweiterung)
41	
42	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-001** (Must) — Jeder Task hat einen berechneten (nicht manuell gesetzten) Zustand "Urgent" ja/nein, basierend auf Restzeit bis Buffer-Deadline (Default-Schwellenwert: < 3 Tage).
43	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-002** (Must) — Nur Tasks im Zustand "Important + Urgent" lösen die volle Eskalationskette (Reminder, ggf. Buddy-Alert) aus. "Important + Not Urgent"-Tasks werden getrackt, aber ohne aktive Benachrichtigung.
44	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-003** (Should) — Der Urgent-Schwellenwert ist in den Settings konfigurierbar (Default-Wert reicht für MVP-Funktionalität).
45	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-004** (Should) — Ein hinterlegter Accountability-Buddy-Kontakt (Settings) wird bei Eskalationsstufe 3 (75%-Checkpoint verpasst / Buffer-Deadline überschritten) für "Important + Urgent"-Tasks automatisch benachrichtigt.
46	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-005** (Should) — Die Buddy-Benachrichtigung enthält nur "Task X braucht Aufmerksamkeit", keine vollen Task-Details.
47	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-006** (Could) — Pro-Task individualisierbare Urgent-Schwellenwerte.
48	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-007** (Could) — Live-Neuberechnung des Eisenhower-Quadranten bei jedem App-Öffnen statt nur im täglichen Cron-Durchlauf.
49

Claude12.07.2026 18:47
Bash
{
  "command": "cd /d/xampp/htdocs/nudge\ngit add core-app/app.py core-app/models.py core-app/templates/all_tasks.html core-app/templates/dashboard.html docs/03-mvp-requirements-checklist.md docs/13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md\ngit commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nMove Add Task to Dashboard, turn All-Tasks into a grouped backlog\n\nImplements ESC-FUNC-001 (calculated is_urgent state, no stored\nfield) and reworks the All-Tasks page around it. Grouped view\n(Urgent / Not Urgent / On Hold & Done) is now the default, with a\nList toggle kept as a secondary view rather than removed. Deliberately\nskipped a real 4-quadrant Eisenhower matrix -- 02b-eisenhower-buddy-\ndesign.md only ever specifies two quadrants (Important is implicit\nfor every task in this system, there's no \"Not Important\" concept\nin the data model), so grouping by Urgent/Not-Urgent alone matches\nwhat's actually specified instead of inventing new scope.\n\nAdd Task moved from All-Tasks to Dashboard; the creation route and\nits semantics are unchanged, only the redirect target and which page\nrenders the form.\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1\ngit push 2>&1",

Claude12.07.2026 18:47

Step 10a is committed and pushed. Summary:

  • [core-app/models.py](core-app/models.py) — added is_urgent() (ESC-FUNC-001), a pure calculated function like is_checkpoint_due(), no new DB column
  • [core-app/app.py](core-app/app.py), [templates/all_tasks.html](core-app/templates/all_tasks.html) — All-Tasks now defaults to a Grouped view (Urgent / Not Urgent / On Hold & Done), with List kept as a secondary toggle
  • [core-app/templates/dashboard.html](core-app/templates/dashboard.html) — Add-Task form moved here from All-Tasks
  • [docs/13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md](docs/13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md)

Verified the whole thing against the seed dataset: Grouped view correctly bucketed all 5 seeded tasks (2 urgent, 1 not-urgent, 2 in on-hold/done), the List toggle works cleanly with no JS errors, the Add-Task form is gone from All-Tasks (replaced with a pointer back to Dashboard), and creating a task from the Dashboard now correctly redirects back to the Dashboard instead of All-Tasks.

Ready for Step 10b: the buddy system (ESC-FUNC-004/005) whenever you want to continue.

Du12.07.2026 18:54

looks good, a couple of things:

I think you should be able to add task from "all tasks" but it should be less noticable, so maybe a round "+" in the right corner that opens a pop up to add a task? is the dashboard element one we can easily "reuse"?

all tasks - list has incorrect spacing between elements. this view should also allow for sorting I think (maybe the grouped view as well?

now that I think about it, without the eisenhower idea behind it, maybe we can have it in one view with a simple toolbar that has sort A/Z, sort date, group/ungroup? not sure.

I'm also keeping two things in mind: if we add this "task dump category" where does it live? and we also briefly talked about tasks being added to projects, which doesn't appear anywhere yet either.

Claude12.07.2026 18:56

Good catch on the reuse question — yes, easily. Let me fix the spacing bug (found the cause) and build the FAB + shared form in one pass, then share my thinking on the bigger open questions.

First, the spacing bug — I see it: the List view's container has flex-col and gap-3 but is missing the base flex class, so gap never actually applies (it only works inside flex/grid containers).

  "old_string": "@app.route(\"/tasks\", methods=[\"GET\", \"POST\"])\ndef all_tasks():\n    if request.method == \"POST\":\n        name = request.form[\"name\"]\n        external_deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(request.form[\"external_deadline\"])\n        create_task(name, external_deadline)\n        return redirect(url_for(\"dashboard\"))",
  "new_string": "@app.route(\"/tasks\", methods=[\"GET\", \"POST\"])\ndef all_tasks():\n    if request.method == \"POST\":\n        name = request.form[\"name\"]\n        external_deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(request.form[\"external_deadline\"])\n        create_task(name, external_deadline)\n        redirect_to = request.form.get(\"redirect_to\", \"dashboard\")\n        if redirect_to not in (\"dashboard\", \"all_tasks\"):\n            redirect_to = \"dashboard\"\n        return redirect(url_for(redirect_to))"
}

  "content": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\n{% from \"_add_task_form.html\" import add_task_form %}\n{% block title %}All Tasks | Nudge{% endblock %}\n{% block content %}\n{% macro task_row(task) %}\n<a href=\"{{ url_for('task_detail', task_id=task.id) }}\"\n   class=\"flex justify-between items-center bg-surface rounded-lg px-5 py-4 hover:shadow-sm transition-shadow\">\n    <div class=\"flex items-center gap-3\">\n        <span class=\"font-medium\">{{ task.name }}</span>\n        {% if task.is_focus_task %}\n        <span class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-accent text-white flex items-center gap-1\">\n            <span class=\"material-symbols-outlined text-[14px]\">flag</span>\n            Focus\n        </span>\n        {% endif %}\n        <span class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full\n            {{ 'bg-primary/30' if task.status == 'active' else 'bg-black/10 text-text/60' }}\">\n            {{ task.status.replace('_', ' ') }}\n        </span>\n    </div>\n    <span class=\"text-sm text-text/60\">{{ task.buffer_deadline|friendly_date }}</span>\n</a>\n{% endmacro %}\n\n<div class=\"flex items-center justify-between mb-8\">\n    <h2 class=\"font-display font-bold text-3xl\">All Tasks</h2>\n    <div class=\"flex bg-surface rounded-lg p-1 gap-1\">\n        <button type=\"button\" onclick=\"showView('grouped')\" id=\"grouped-toggle\"\n                class=\"px-4 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold rounded-md transition-colors bg-accent text-white\">\n            Grouped\n        </button>\n        <button type=\"button\" onclick=\"showView('list')\" id=\"list-toggle\"\n                class=\"px-4 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold rounded-md transition-colors text-text/60\">\n            List\n        </button>\n    </div>\n</div>\n\n<div id=\"grouped-view\" class=\"flex flex-col gap-8 mb-10 max-w-2xl\">\n    <div>\n        <h3 class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-text/60 font-semibold mb-3 flex items-center gap-2\">\n            <span class=\"w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-red-500\"></span>\n            Urgent\n        </h3>\n        <div class=\"flex flex-col gap-3\">\n            {% for task in urgent_tasks %}\n            {{ task_row(task) }}\n            {% else %}\n            <p class=\"text-text/50 text-sm\">Nothing urgent right now.</p>\n            {% endfor %}\n        </div>\n    </div>\n    <div>\n        <h3 class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-text/60 font-semibold mb-3 flex items-center gap-2\">\n            <span class=\"w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary\"></span>\n            Not Urgent\n        </h3>\n        <div class=\"flex flex-col gap-3\">\n            {% for task in not_urgent_tasks %}\n            {{ task_row(task) }}\n            {% else %}\n            <p class=\"text-text/50 text-sm\">Nothing here.</p>\n            {% endfor %}\n        </div>\n    </div>\n    {% if other_tasks %}\n    <div class=\"opacity-70\">\n        <h3 class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-text/60 font-semibold mb-3 flex items-center gap-2\">\n            <span class=\"w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-black/30\"></span>\n            On Hold &amp; Done\n        </h3>\n        <div class=\"flex flex-col gap-3\">\n            {% for task in other_tasks %}\n            {{ task_row(task) }}\n            {% endfor %}\n        </div>\n    </div>\n    {% endif %}\n</div>\n\n<div id=\"list-view\" class=\"hidden flex-col gap-3 mb-10 max-w-2xl\">\n    {% for task in tasks %}\n    {{ task_row(task) }}\n    {% else %}\n    <p class=\"text-text/60\">No tasks yet — use the + button to add one.</p>\n    {% endfor %}\n</div>\n\n<!-- Floating Action Button -->\n<button type=\"button\" onclick=\"document.getElementById('add-task-modal').classList.remove('hidden')\"\n        class=\"fixed bottom-8 right-8 w-14 h-14 bg-accent text-white rounded-full shadow-lg flex items-center justify-center hover:scale-105 active:scale-95 transition-transform\">\n    <span class=\"material-symbols-outlined text-3xl\">add</span>\n</button>\n\n<!-- Add Task Modal -->\n<div id=\"add-task-modal\" class=\"hidden fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center p-6\">\n    <div class=\"absolute inset-0 bg-black/40\"\n         onclick=\"document.getElementById('add-task-modal').classList.add('hidden')\"></div>\n    <div class=\"relative bg-surface rounded-xl p-6 max-w-md w-full\">\n        <h3 class=\"font-display font-semibold text-lg mb-4\">Add a task</h3>\n        {{ add_task_form('all_tasks') }}\n    </div>\n</div>\n\n<script>\n    function showView(view) {\n        const grouped = document.getElementById('grouped-view');\n        const list = document.getElementById('list-view');\n        const groupedToggle = document.getElementById('grouped-toggle');\n        const listToggle = document.getElementById('list-toggle');\n        const active = ['bg-accent', 'text-white'];\n        const inactive = ['text-text/60'];\n\n        if (view === 'grouped') {\n            grouped.classList.remove('hidden');\n            grouped.classList.add('flex');\n            list.classList.add('hidden');\n            list.classList.remove('flex');\n            groupedToggle.classList.add(...active);\n            groupedToggle.classList.remove(...inactive);\n            listToggle.classList.remove(...active);\n            listToggle.classList.add(...inactive);\n        } else {\n            list.classList.remove('hidden');\n            list.classList.add('flex');\n            grouped.classList.add('hidden');\n            grouped.classList.remove('flex');\n            listToggle.classList.add(...active);\n            listToggle.classList.remove(...inactive);\n            groupedToggle.classList.remove(...active);\n            groupedToggle.classList.add(...inactive);\n        }\n    }\n</script>\n{% endblock %}\n"
}

I think you should be able to add task from "all tasks" but it should be less noticable, so maybe a round "+" in the right corner that opens a pop up to add a task? is the dashboard element one we can easily "reuse"?

all tasks - list has incorrect spacing between elements. this view should also allow for sorting I think (maybe the grouped view as well?

now that I think about it, without the eisenhower idea behind it, maybe we can have it in one view with a simple toolbar that has sort A/Z, sort date, group/ungroup? not sure.

1	{% extends "base.html" %}
2	{% block title %}Focus Timer: {{ task.name }} | Nudge{% endblock %}
3	{% block content %}
4	<a href="{{ url_for('task_detail', task_id=task.id) }}" class="text-sm text-text/60 hover:text-text mb-6 inline-block">
5	    &larr; Back to Task
6	</a>
7	
8	<div class="max-w-md text-center mx-auto mt-10">
9	    <p class="text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-text/60 mb-2">Focus sprint</p>
10	    <h2 class="font-display font-bold text-2xl mb-8">{{ task.name }}</h2>
11	    <div id="timer-display" class="font-display font-bold text-6xl text-accent mb-8">10:00</div>
12	    <p id="timer-message" class="text-text/60">Just get started — you can stop anytime.</p>
13	</div>
14	<script>
15	    let secondsLeft = 10 * 60;
16	    const display = document.getElementById('timer-display');
17	    const message = document.getElementById('timer-message');
18	
19	    const interval = setInterval(() => {
20	        secondsLeft -= 1;
21	        const minutes = Math.floor(secondsLeft / 60);
22	        const seconds = secondsLeft % 60;
23	        display.textContent = `${minutes}:${seconds.toString().padStart(2, '0')}`;
24	        if (secondsLeft <= 0) {
25	            clearInterval(interval);
26	            display.textContent = "0:00";
27	            message.textContent = "Time's up — nice work getting started.";
28	        }
29	    }, 1000);
30	</script>
31	{% endblock %}
32

Claude12.07.2026 19:21

Both fixed:


  • Timer → Back: now points back to the Triage selection screen instead of jumping to the task detail page.

  • Selected triage option: the three reason buttons now get a visible accent border when clicked, using a data-reason attribute + class toggle — clears when you pick a different one.

Since your server's running with debug=True, Flask's reloader should pick up both edits automatically — just refresh.

Now, catching up on the earlier feedback since I'd already built it before the interruption: the "+" FAB and modal on All Tasks is done (reuses the exact same form as Dashboard via a shared Jinja macro — that's the "easily reuse" answer: it's just one <form> definition imported into both pages, with a hidden field telling the backend which page to redirect back to after creating a task). The List-view spacing bug is also fixed — the container was missing the flex display class, so Tailwind's gap utility had nothing to apply to.

On your three open questions — these read more like you're thinking out loud than settled asks, so here's where I'd land rather than just building blind:

Sorting toolbar (A-Z / Date / Group-ungroup): I like the instinct — it's a real simplification. But it changes what we just built from "here's your urgent stuff" (a specific lens) to "sort however you want" (a neutral utility), which is a genuine reframe, not just an addition. I'd hold off until buddy-system (10b) is done, then revisit both together, since sorting and grouping are the same view.

"Task dump" category and Projects — where do they live: Neither is in your requirements checklist. Worth flagging: "Projects" specifically showed up before only as part of the Stitch-generated GTD nav (Today / Inbox / Projects / Archive) that we explicitly rejected in the design audit as generic-SaaS drift Nudge shouldn't inherit — it was never a feature we agreed to build. If you want a real lightweight grouping concept now, that's a legitimate new idea, but it's a different thing than resurrecting the rejected nav item. Same for task-dump: it's the same shape as the deferred "deadline-less tasks" backlog entry. I'd keep both parked in backlog.md until the MVP's done, given the assignment deadline — happy to actually scope either one out properly whenever you want, just didn't want to silently expand scope on a hunch.

before we continue 10b (because I think this would be a good place to implement settings as well) -- what else does the backlog have?

one thing I want to note is date/time formats and language (language might be a bit overboard but changing the date/time in settings would give more utility I think).

1	# Backlog: Bewusst zurückgestellte Ideen
2	
3	Sammelstelle für Punkte, die während des Baus aufkamen, aber bewusst nicht in den MVP-Scope gezogen wurden — kein Vollständigkeits-Versäumnis, sondern dokumentierte Entscheidung (siehe `03-mvp-requirements-checklist.md`, Abschnitt "Bewusst nicht atomarisiert" für dasselbe Prinzip). Jeder Eintrag verlinkt zurück zu der Stelle, an der er aufkam, für den vollen Kontext. Lebendes Dokument — bei Bedarf ergänzen, bei Umsetzung hierher als "erledigt" markieren oder entfernen.
4	
5	---
6	
7	- [ ] **HTML-formatierte Review-Mail** statt reinem Plaintext (`notification-worker/worker.py`, `build_review_email`). Aufgeworfen nach Schritt 6. Würde z. B. echte visuelle Blöcke statt Trennlinien-Zeichen, anklickbare Task-Links zurück in die Kern-App etc. ermöglichen. Reiner Plaintext war für den MVP bewusst einfacher (kein HTML-Templating im Worker nötig, in jedem Mail-Client lesbar, leicht in der Prüfung zu erklären).
8	
9	- [ ] **14-Tage-Fenster + Cap bei 10 dringendsten Tasks** für die Review-Mail, falls sich in der Praxis zeigt, dass die reine Block-Formatierung (Schritt 6) bei sehr vielen offenen Tasks nicht ausreicht. Siehe `09-step6-email-and-mailpit.md`, Abschnitt "Nachtrag aus der Nutzerinnen-Review" für die volle Abwägung gegen REVIEW-FUNC-003.
10	
11	- [ ] **Deadline-lose Tasks** (Backlog-/Ideen-Objekttyp, nimmt nicht am Checkpoint-/Buffer-/Eisenhower-Mechanismus teil). Erste Designidee: automatischer Wiedervorlage-Reminder ca. einen Monat nach Anlage. Siehe `05-step2-task-model.md`, Abschnitt "Scope-Frage während Review".
12	
13	- [ ] **KI-gestütztes Reply-Parsing** (lokales Modell via Ollama) als dritter, unabhängiger Prozess, ergänzend zum regelbasierten Parsing aus Schritt 7 — nicht als Ersatz. Siehe `00-kickoff-briefing.md`, Abschnitt 7.
14	
15	- [ ] **Settings-Screen** (SMTP-Konfiguration fürs UI statt hartkodierter Konstanten in `worker.py`, Checkpoint-Intervall-Overrides, Urgent-Schwellenwert, Accountability-Buddy-Kon
… [truncated, 1556 more chars]

Siehe `11-step8-focus-task-writeback.md`.\n\n- [ ] **Sortier-/Gruppier-Toolbar für All-Tasks** (Sort A-Z, Sort nach Datum, Group/Ungroup) statt der festen Grouped/List-Umschaltung aus Schritt 10a. Aufgeworfen direkt nach Schritt 10a — bewusst zurückgestellt, bis Schritt 10b (Buddy-System) abgeschlossen ist, dann gemeinsam mit der bestehenden Gruppierung überarbeiten, da beides dieselbe Ansicht betrifft.\n\n- [ ] **Listen/Projekt-Tags über Quick-Add-Syntax** (z. B. `#project`), inspiriert von einer Referenz-App, die dafür \"Lists\" statt \"Projects\" nutzt (siehe `design-reference/inspiration/`). Ursprünglich aufgeworfen als Teil der Quick-Add-Parser-Diskussion in `02c-screen-audit-and-design-decisions.md`, Abschnitt 5 (dort nur der Termin-Teil des Parsers zurückgestellt, der Tagging-Teil war noch nicht explizit benannt). Kein \"Projects\"-Feature wie im verworfenen Stitch-Nav — eher ein leichtgewichtiges Gruppierungslabel auf Task-Ebene. Noch nicht spezifiziert, wie Listen/Tags im Datenmodell aussehen würden.\n\n- [ ] **Konfigurierbares Datum-/Zeitformat** in den Settings, sobald es einen Settings-Screen gibt (siehe oben). Aktuell fest in `friendly_date()` (`core-app/app.py`) auf ein Format hartkodiert.\n\n- [ ] **Sprache/Lokalisierung** — von der Nutzerin selbst als vermutlich zu groß für den MVP-Rahmen eingestuft (\"könnte übertrieben sein\"), hier nur als Idee vermerkt, keine Priorität zugewiesen."

Claude12.07.2026 19:27

Updated the backlog with the corrected context on the Lists/tags idea, plus your two new items. Here's the full current list, so you can decide what to fold into 10b's Settings work:

Settings-relevant (directly actionable in 10b):


  • Settings screen itself — SMTP config, checkpoint-interval overrides, urgent threshold, buddy contact (currently all hardcoded constants or missing)

  • Configurable date/time format (currently hardcoded in friendly_date())

  • Language/localization — flagged by you as probably too big for MVP, no priority assigned

Sorting/grouping (deferred until after 10b, same view):


  • Sort A-Z / Sort by date / Group-ungroup toolbar for All-Tasks

  • Dashboard sorting by focus-task status (currently badge-only, doesn't affect order)

Data-model-shaped ideas (bigger, unscoped):


  • Deadline-less tasks (backlog/idea object type)

  • Lists/project tags via quick-add syntax (#project) — the one we just clarified

Worker/email:


  • HTML-formatted review email

  • 14-day-window + top-10 cap (alternative to the block formatting)

  • AI-assisted reply-parsing via Ollama (third process, complementary)

Smaller/polish:


  • Worker /health endpoint

  • Attachment renaming

  • Docker (portfolio polish only)

  • ESC-FUNC-006 (per-task urgent thresholds), ESC-FUNC-007 (live quadrant recalc)

Given you want Settings built as part of 10b, the two directly relevant ones are the Settings screen itself and the date/time format. Want me to fold both into 10b's scope, or keep date/time format separate/later?

Du12.07.2026 19:29

then let's do settings now and then the buddy system after.

Claude12.07.2026 19:31

That's a substantial but well-defined piece of work. The key architectural call: since core-app is the only process with a UI, Settings has to live in its database, and the worker polls a new /api/settings endpoint the same way it already polls /api/tasks — no new pattern, just extending the one that's already there. Let me build this out.

1	import sqlite3
2	from pathlib import Path
3	
4	DB_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "nudge.db"
5	
6	
7	def get_connection():
8	    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
9	    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
10	    return conn
11	
12	
13	def init_db():
14	    conn = get_connection()
15	    conn.execute(
16	        """
17	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
18	            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
19	            name TEXT NOT NULL,
20	            created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
21	            external_deadline TEXT NOT NULL,
22	            buffer_deadline TEXT NOT NULL,
23	            checkpoint_1 TEXT NOT NULL,
24	            checkpoint_2 TEXT NOT NULL,
25	            status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
26	            is_focus_task INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
27	        )
28	        """
29	    )
30	    conn.execute(
31	        """
32	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_events (
33	            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
34	            task_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id),
35	            event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
36	            description TEXT NOT NULL,
37	            created_at TEXT NOT NULL
38	        )
39	        """
40	    )
41	    conn.commit()
42	    conn.close()
43

Tool result
1	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
2	
3	from db import get_connection
4	
5	BUFFER_RATIO = 0.7
6	CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO = 0.5
7	CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO = 0.75
8	URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 3
9	
10	
11	def calculate_buffer_deadline(created_at: datetime, external_deadline: datetime) -> datetime:
12	    available_time = external_deadline - created_at
13	    return created_at + BUFFER_RATIO * available_time
14	
15	
16	def calculate_checkpoints(created_at: datetime, buffer_deadline: datetime) -> tuple[datetime, datetime]:
17	    buffer_window = buffer_deadline - created_at
18	    checkpoint_1 = created_at + CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO * buffer_window
19	    checkpoint_2 = created_at + CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO * buffer_window
20	    return checkpoint_1, checkpoint_2
21	
22	
23	def create_task(
24	    name: str,
25	    external_deadline: datetime,
26	    buffer_deadline: datetime | None = None,
27	    checkpoint_1: datetime | None = None,
28	    checkpoint_2: datetime | None = None,
29	) -> dict:
30	    created_at = datetime.now()
31	
32	    if buffer_deadline is None:
33	        buffer_deadline = calculate_buffer_deadline(created_at, external_deadline)
34	
35	    calc_checkpoint_1, calc_checkpoint_2 = calculate_checkpoints(created_at, buffer_deadline)
36	    if checkpoint_1 is None:
37	        checkpoint_1 = calc_checkpoint_1
38	    if checkpoint_2 is None:
39	        checkpoint_2 = calc_checkpoint_2
40	
41	    conn = get_connection()
42	    cursor = conn.execute(
43	        """
44	        INSERT INTO tasks (name, created_at, external_deadline, buffer_deadline, checkpoint_1, checkpoint_2, status)
45	        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'active')
46	        """,
47	        (
48	            name,
49	            created_at.isoformat(),
50	            external_deadline.isoformat(),
51	            buffer_deadline.isoformat(),
52	            checkpoint_1.isoformat(),
53	            checkpoint_2.isoformat(),
54	        ),
55	    )
56	    conn.commit()
57	    task_id = cursor.lastrowid
58	    conn.close

1	from datetime import datetime
2	
3	from flask import Flask, abort, jsonify, redirect, render_template, request, send_from_directory, url_for
4	from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename
5	
6	from db import init_db
7	from models import (
8	    add_task_event,
9	    buffer_progress_percent,
10	    create_task,
11	    get_all_tasks,
12	    get_task,
13	    get_task_events,
14	    is_checkpoint_due,
15	    is_urgent,
16	    set_focus_tasks,
17	    update_task_status,
18	)
19	from uploads import delete_attachment, list_attachments, save_attachment, task_upload_dir
20	
21	app = Flask(__name__)
22	init_db()
23	
24	
25	def friendly_date(value):
26	    if not value:
27	        return ""
28	    return datetime.fromisoformat(value).strftime("%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p")
29	
30	
31	app.jinja_env.filters["friendly_date"] = friendly_date
32	
33	
34	@app.route("/health")
35	def health():
36	    return jsonify(status="ok", service="core-app")
37	
38	
39	# --- HTML pages ---
40	
41	
42	@app.route("/")
43	def dashboard():
44	    active_tasks = [t for t in get_all_tasks() if t["status"] == "active"]
45	    next_task = active_tasks[0] if active_tasks else None
46	    other_tasks = active_tasks[1:]
47	    return render_template("dashboard.html", next_task=next_task, other_tasks=other_tasks)
48	
49	
50	@app.route("/tasks", methods=["GET", "POST"])
51	def all_tasks():
52	    if request.method == "POST":
53	        name = request.form["name"]

17	CORE_APP_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:3000"
18	POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 30
19	
20	SMTP_HOST = "localhost"
21	SMTP_PORT = 1025
22	FROM_ADDRESS = "nudge@localhost"
23	TO_ADDRESS = "user@localhost"
24	REVIEW_EMAIL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 60  # stands in for "weekly" during local testing
25	
26	# Mailpit has no IMAP server (confirmed via `mailpit --help`) — only SMTP,
27	# POP3, and its own REST API. REVIEW-FUNC-004/REVIEW-NFR-001 name IMAP
28	# specifically, but poplib is the closest available stdlib equivalent that
29	# still talks a real mail-retrieval protocol to a real server rather than a
30	# REST mock. See docs/how-to-run-and-test-locally.md for how to enable
31	# Mailpit's POP3 server with matching credentials.
32	POP3_HOST = "localhost"
33	POP3_PORT = 1110
34	POP3_USERNAME = "nudge"
35	POP3_PASSWORD = "nudge-local-test"
36	
37	REVIEW_SUBJECT_PATTERN = re.compile(r"Nudge Weekly Review \[ids: ([\d,]+)\]")
38	TOP_PRIORITY_COUNT = 3
39	
40	processed_reply_uids: set[str] = set()
41	
42

52	    except urllib.error.URLError as error:
53	        log(f"Could not reach core-app at {CORE_APP_URL}: {error}")
54	        return None

Let's start with the schema.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\core-app\\db.py",
  "old_string": "    conn.execute(\n        \"\"\"\n        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_events (\n            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n            task_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id),\n            event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n            description TEXT NOT NULL,\n            created_at TEXT NOT NULL\n        )\n        \"\"\"\n    )\n    conn.commit()\n    conn.close()",
  "new_string": "    conn.execute(\n        \"\"\"\n        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_events (\n            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n            task_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id),\n            event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n            description TEXT NOT NULL,\n            created_at TEXT NOT NULL\n        )\n        \"\"\"\n    )\n    conn.execute(\n        \"\"\"\n        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS settings (\n            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),\n            smtp_host TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'localhost',\n            smtp_port INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1025,\n            from_address TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'nudge@localhost',\n            to_address TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user@localhost',\n            checkpoint_1_ratio REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.5,\n            checkpoint_2_ratio REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.75,\n            urgent_threshold_days REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 3,\n            buddy_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n            buddy_email TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n            date_format TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p'\n        )\n        \"\"\"\n    )\n    # Singleton row (CHECK id = 1 enforces this at the schema level) --\n    # INSERT OR IGNORE means this is a no-op on every init after the first.\n    conn.execute(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO settings (id) VALUES (1)\")\n    conn.commit()\n    conn.close()"
}

Now models.py — settings CRUD, and updating the calculation functions to accept ratios explicitly.

Claude12.07.2026 19:31
Edit
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\core-app\\models.py",
  "old_string": "def calculate_buffer_deadline(created_at: datetime, external_deadline: datetime) -> datetime:\n    available_time = external_deadline - created_at\n    return created_at + BUFFER_RATIO * available_time\n\n\ndef calculate_checkpoints(created_at: datetime, buffer_deadline: datetime) -> tuple[datetime, datetime]:\n    buffer_window = buffer_deadline - created_at\n    checkpoint_1 = created_at + CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO * buffer_window\n    checkpoint_2 = created_at + CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO * buffer_window\n    return checkpoint_1, checkpoint_2\n\n\ndef create_task(\n    name: str,\n    external_deadline: datetime,\n    buffer_deadline: datetime | None = None,\n    checkpoint_1: datetime | None = None,\n    checkpoint_2: datetime | None = None,\n) -> dict:\n    created_at = datetime.now()\n\n    if buffer_deadline is None:\n        buffer_deadline = calculate_buffer_deadline(created_at, external_deadline)\n\n    calc_checkpoint_1, calc_checkpoint_2 = calculate_checkpoints(created_at, buffer_deadline)\n    if checkpoint_1 is None:\n        checkpoint_1 = calc_checkpoint_1\n    if checkpoint_2 is None:\n        checkpoint_2 = calc_checkpoint_2",
  "new_string": "def calculate_buffer_deadline(\n    created_at: datetime, external_deadline: datetime, buffer_ratio: float = BUFFER_RATIO\n) -> datetime:\n    available_time = external_deadline - created_at\n    return created_at + buffer_ratio * available_time\n\n\ndef calculate_checkpoints(\n    created_at: datetime,\n    buffer_deadline: datetime,\n    checkpoint_1_ratio: float = CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO,\n    checkpoint_2_ratio: float = CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO,\n) -> tuple[datetime, datetime]:\n    buffer_window = buffer_deadline - created_at\n    checkpoint_1 = created_at + checkpoint_1_ratio * buffer_window\n    checkpoint_2 = created_at + checkpoint_2_ratio * buffer_window\n    return checkpoint_1, checkpoint_2\n\n\ndef create_task(\n    name: str,\n    external_deadline: datetime,\n    buffer_deadline: datetime | None = None,\n    checkpoint_1: datetime | None = None,\n    checkpoint_2: datetime | None = None,\n) -> dict:\n    created_at = datetime.now()\n    settings = get_settings()\n\n    if buffer_deadline is None:\n        buffer_deadline = calculate_buffer_deadline(created_at, external_deadline)\n\n    calc_checkpoint_1, calc_checkpoint_2 = calculate_checkpoints(\n        created_at, buffer_deadline, settings[\"checkpoint_1_ratio\"], settings[\"checkpoint_2_ratio\"]\n    )\n    if checkpoint_1 is None:\n        checkpoint_1 = calc_checkpoint_1\n    if checkpoint_2 is None:\n        checkpoint_2 = calc_checkpoint_2"
}

Claude12.07.2026 19:31

Now let's add get_settings()/update_settings() and update is_urgent() to accept an explicit threshold.

Claude12.07.2026 19:31
Edit
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\core-app\\models.py",
  "old_string": "def is_urgent(task: dict) -> bool:\n    if task[\"status\"] != \"active\":\n        return False\n    buffer_deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(task[\"buffer_deadline\"])\n    return buffer_deadline - datetime.now() < timedelta(days=URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS)",
  "new_string": "def is_urgent(task: dict, threshold_days: float = URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS) -> bool:\n    if task[\"status\"] != \"active\":\n        return False\n    buffer_deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(task[\"buffer_deadline\"])\n    return buffer_deadline - datetime.now() < timedelta(days=threshold_days)\n\n\ndef get_settings() -> dict:\n    conn = get_connection()\n    row = conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM settings WHERE id = 1\").fetchone()\n    conn.close()\n    return dict(row)\n\n\ndef update_settings(**fields) -> dict:\n    conn = get_connection()\n    assignments = \", \".join(f\"{key} = ?\" for key in fields)\n    conn.execute(f\"UPDATE settings SET {assignments} WHERE id = 1\", list(fields.values()))\n    conn.commit()\n    conn.close()\n    return get_settings()"
}
Tool result
The file D:\xampp\htdocs\nudge\core-app\models.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Now let's update app.py — the settings routes, the g-based per-request settings cache, and wiring is_urgent to the configured threshold.

Claude12.07.2026 19:32
Edit
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\core-app\\app.py",
  "old_string": "from datetime import datetime\n\nfrom flask import Flask, abort, jsonify, redirect, render_template, request, send_from_directory, url_for\nfrom werkzeug.utils import secure_filename\n\nfrom db import init_db\nfrom models import (\n    add_task_event,\n    buffer_progress_percent,\n    create_task,\n    get_all_tasks,\n    get_task,\n    get_task_events,\n    is_checkpoint_due,\n    is_urgent,\n    set_focus_tasks,\n    update_task_status,\n)\nfrom uploads import delete_attachment, list_attachments, save_attachment, task_upload_dir\n\napp = Flask(__name__)\ninit_db()\n\n\ndef friendly_date(value):\n    if not value:\n        return \"\"\n    return datetime.fromisoformat(value).strftime(\"%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p\")\n\n\napp.jinja_env.filters[\"friendly_date\"] = friendly_date",
  "new_string": "from datetime import datetime\n\nfrom flask import Flask, abort, g, jsonify, redirect, render_template, request, send_from_directory, url_for\nfrom werkzeug.utils import secure_filename\n\nfrom db import init_db\nfrom models import (\n    add_task_event,\n    buffer_progress_percent,\n    create_task,\n    get_all_tasks,\n    get_settings,\n    get_task,\n    get_task_events,\n    is_checkpoint_due,\n    is_urgent,\n    set_focus_tasks,\n    update_settings,\n    update_task_status,\n)\nfrom uploads import delete_attachment, list_attachments, save_attachment, task_upload_dir\n\napp = Flask(__name__)\ninit_db()\n\n\n@app.before_request\ndef load_settings():\n    # Cached once per request in Flask's request-scoped `g` object so\n    # every date rendered on a page (potentially dozens on All-Tasks)\n    # doesn't hit the database individually.\n    g.settings = get_settings()\n\n\ndef friendly_date(value):\n    if not value:\n        return \"\"\n    date_format = g.settings[\"date_format\"] if \"settings\" in g else \"%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p\"\n    return datetime.fromisoformat(value).strftime(date_format)\n\n\napp.jinja_env.filters[\"friendly_date\"] = friendly_date"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\core-app\\templates\\settings.html",
  "content": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\n{% block title %}Settings | Nudge{% endblock %}\n{% block content %}\n<h2 class=\"font-display font-bold text-3xl mb-8\">Settings</h2>\n\n<form method=\"post\" action=\"{{ url_for('settings') }}\" class=\"flex flex-col gap-6 max-w-2xl\">\n    <section class=\"bg-surface rounded-xl p-8\">\n        <h3 class=\"font-display font-semibold text-lg mb-1\">Email</h3>\n        <p class=\"text-sm text-text/60 mb-6\">\n            Used by the notification-worker to send the weekly review email (SMTP only — never used to read mail).\n        </p>\n        <div class=\"grid grid-cols-2 gap-4\">\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">SMTP host</label>\n                <input type=\"text\" name=\"smtp_host\" value=\"{{ settings.smtp_host }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">SMTP port</label>\n                <input type=\"number\" name=\"smtp_port\" value=\"{{ settings.smtp_port }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">From address</label>\n                <input type=\"email\" name=\"from_address\" value=\"{{ settings.from_address }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">To address</label>\n                <input type=\"email\" name=\"to_address\" value=\"{{ settings.to_address }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </section>\n\n    <section class=\"bg-surface rounded-xl p-8\">\n        <h3 class=\"font-display font-semibold text-lg mb-1\">Checkpoints</h3>\n        <p class=\"text-sm text-text/60 mb-6\">\n            Default checkpoint spacing for new tasks, as a percentage of the buffer window (creation → buffer deadline).\n            Existing tasks keep the values they were created with.\n        </p>\n        <div class=\"grid grid-cols-2 gap-4\">\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">Checkpoint 1 (%)</label>\n                <input type=\"number\" name=\"checkpoint_1_percent\" min=\"1\" max=\"99\" step=\"1\"\n                       value=\"{{ (settings.checkpoint_1_ratio * 100) | round | int }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">Checkpoint 2 (%)</label>\n                <input type=\"number\" name=\"checkpoint_2_percent\" min=\"1\" max=\"99\" step=\"1\"\n                       value=\"{{ (settings.checkpoint_2_ratio * 100) | round | int }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </section>\n\n    <section class=\"bg-surface rounded-xl p-8\">\n        <h3 class=\"font-display font-semibold text-lg mb-1\">Escalation</h3>\n        <p class=\"text-sm text-text/60 mb-6\">\n            When a task counts as \"urgent\" (All-Tasks grouping), and who gets notified if it stays unresolved.\n        </p>\n        <div class=\"grid grid-cols-2 gap-4\">\n            <div class=\"col-span-2\">\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">\n                    Urgent threshold (days before buffer deadline)\n                </label>\n                <input type=\"number\" name=\"urgent_threshold_days\" min=\"0\" step=\"0.5\"\n                       value=\"{{ settings.urgent_threshold_days }}\" required\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">Buddy name</label>\n                <input type=\"text\" name=\"buddy_name\" value=\"{{ settings.buddy_name }}\" placeholder=\"Optional\"\n                       class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n            <div>\n                <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </section>\n\n    <section class=\"bg-surface rounded-xl p-8\">\n        <h3 class=\"font-display font-semibold text-lg mb-1\">Display</h3>\n        <p class=\"text-sm text-text/60 mb-6\">How dates and times are shown throughout the app.</p>\n        <label class=\"text-xs uppercase tracking-wide font-semibold text-text/70 block mb-1\">Date format</label>\n        <select name=\"date_format\"\n                class=\"w-full border-b border-black/20 focus:border-accent outline-none py-2 bg-transparent\">\n            <option value=\"%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p\" {{ 'selected' if settings.date_format == '%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p' }}>\n                Jul 12, 2026 11:08 AM\n            </option>\n            <option value=\"%d %b %Y, %H:%M\" {{ 'selected' if settings.date_format == '%d %b %Y, %H:%M' }}>\n                12 Jul 2026, 11:08\n text-white font-semibold px-6 py-3 rounded-lg self-start\">\n        Save Settings\n    </button>\n</form>\n{% endblock %}\n"

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\notification-worker\\worker.py",
  "old_string": "CORE_APP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:3000\"\nPOLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 30\n\nSMTP_HOST = \"localhost\"\nSMTP_PORT = 1025\nFROM_ADDRESS = \"nudge@localhost\"\nTO_ADDRESS = \"user@localhost\"\nREVIEW_EMAIL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 60  # stands in for \"weekly\" during local testing",
  "new_string": "CORE_APP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:3000\"\nPOLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 30\nREVIEW_EMAIL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 60  # stands in for \"weekly\" during local testing\n\n# Fallback used only if core-app's /api/settings can't be reached -- the\n# worker should still degrade gracefully rather than crash for lack of\n# settings, same resilience pattern as fetch_open_tasks().\nDEFAULT_SETTINGS = {\n    \"smtp_host\": \"localhost\",\n    \"smtp_port\": 1025,\n    \"from_address\": \"nudge@localhost\",\n    \"to_address\": \"user@localhost\",\n}"
}

52	def fetch_open_tasks() -> list[dict] | None:
53	    url = f"{CORE_APP_URL}/api/tasks"
54	    try:
55	        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=5) as response:
56	            tasks = json.loads(response.read())
57	    except urllib.error.URLError as error:
58	        log(f"Could not reach core-app at {CORE_APP_URL}: {error}")
59	        return None

64	def post_focus_tasks(task_ids: list[int]) -> None:
65	    url = f"{CORE_APP_URL}/api/tasks/focus"
66	    payload = json.dumps({"task_ids": task_ids}).encode("utf-8")
67	    request_obj = urllib.request.Request(
68	        url, data=payload, headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}, method="POST"
69	    )

71	        with urllib.request.urlopen(request_obj, timeout=5) as response:
72	            result = json.loads(response.read())

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\notification-worker\\worker.py",
  "old_string": "    return [task for task in tasks if task[\"status\"] == \"active\"]\n\n\ndef post_focus_tasks(task_ids: list[int]) -> None:",
  "new_string": "    return [task for task in tasks if task[\"status\"] == \"active\"]\n\n\ndef fetch_settings() -> dict:\n    url = f\"{CORE_APP_URL}/api/settings\"\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=5) as response:\n            return json.loads(response.read())\n    except urllib.error.URLError as error:\n        log(f\"Could not fetch settings from core-app at {url}, using defaults: {error}\")\n        return DEFAULT_SETTINGS\n\n\ndef post_focus_tasks(task_ids: list[int]) -> None:"
}

105	TASKS_PER_BLOCK = 5
106	
107	
108	def build_review_email(tasks: list[dict]) -> MIMEText:
109	    task_ids = ",".join(str(task["id"]) for task in tasks)
110	    subject = f"Nudge Weekly Review [ids: {task_ids}]"
111	
112	    # tasks arrive sorted by urgency (core-app orders /api/tasks by buffer
113	    # deadline), so grouping them in fixed-size blocks as-is means each
114	    # block is also roughly a tier of urgency, not an arbitrary chunk.
115	    lines = ["Here are your currently open tasks:", ""]
116	    for index, task in enumerate(tasks):
117	        if index > 0 and index % TASKS_PER_BLOCK == 0:
118	            lines.append("-" * 30)
119	            lines.append("")
120	        lines.append(f"#{task['id']} — {task['name']}")
121	        lines.append(f"    Status: {task['status']}")
122	        lines.append(f"    Next checkpoint: {format_date(next_checkpoint(task))}")
123	        lines.append("")
124	    lines.append("Reply with the IDs of your top 3 priorities for this week.")
125	
126	    message = MIMEText("\n".join(lines))
127	    message["Subject"] = subject
128	    message["From"] = FROM_ADDRESS
129	    message["To"] = TO_ADDRESS
130	    message["Message-ID"] = make_msgid()
131	    return message
132	
133	
134	def send_review_email(tasks: list[dict]) -> None:
135	    if not tasks:
136	        log("No open tasks — skipping weekly review email.")
137	        return
138	
139	    message = build_review_email(tasks)
140	    try:
141	        with smtplib.SMTP(SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, timeout=5) as server:
142	            server.send_message(message)
143	    except OSError as error:
144	        log(f"Could not send weekly review email via {SMTP_HOST}:{SMTP_PORT}: {error}")
145	        return
146	
147	    log(f"Sent weekly review email ({len(tasks)} task(s)), subject: {message['Subject']}")
148	
149
Claude12.07.2026 19:33
Edit
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\notification-worker\\worker.py",
  "old_string": "def build_review_email(tasks: list[dict]) -> MIMEText:\n    task_ids = \",\".join(str(task[\"id\"]) for task in tasks)\n    subject = f\"Nudge Weekly Review [ids: {task_ids}]\"\n\n    # tasks arrive sorted by urgency (core-app orders /api/tasks by buffer\n    # deadline), so grouping them in fixed-size blocks as-is means each\n    # block is also roughly a tier of urgency, not an arbitrary chunk.\n    lines = [\"Here are your currently open tasks:\", \"\"]\n    for index, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        if index > 0 and index % TASKS_PER_BLOCK == 0:\n            lines.append(\"-\" * 30)\n            lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"#{task['id']} — {task['name']}\")\n        lines.append(f\"    Status: {task['status']}\")\n        lines.append(f\"    Next checkpoint: {format_date(next_checkpoint(task))}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    lines.append(\"Reply with the IDs of your top 3 priorities for this week.\")\n\n    message = MIMEText(\"\\n\".join(lines))\n    message[\"Subject\"] = subject\n    message[\"From\"] = FROM_ADDRESS\n    message[\"To\"] = TO_ADDRESS\n    message[\"Message-ID\"] = make_msgid()\n    return message\n\n\ndef send_review_email(tasks: list[dict]) -> None:\n    if not tasks:\n        log(\"No open tasks — skipping weekly review email.\")\n        return\n\n    message = build_review_email(tasks)\n    try:\n        with smtplib.SMTP(SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, timeout=5) as server:\n            server.send_message(message)\n    except OSError as error:\n        log(f\"Could not send weekly review email via {SMTP_HOST}:{SMTP_PORT}: {error}\")\n        return\n\n    log(f\"Sent weekly review email ({len(tasks)} task(s)), subject: {message['Subject']}\")",
  "new_string": "def build_review_email(tasks: list[dict], settings: dict) -> MIMEText:\n    task_ids = \",\".join(str(task[\"id\"]) for task in tasks)\n    subject = f\"Nudge Weekly Review [ids: {task_ids}]\"\n\n    # tasks arrive sorted by urgency (core-app orders /api/tasks by buffer\n    # deadline), so grouping them in fixed-size blocks as-is means each\n    # block is also roughly a tier of urgency, not an arbitrary chunk.\n    lines = [\"Here are your currently open tasks:\", \"\"]\n    for index, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        if index > 0 and index % TASKS_PER_BLOCK == 0:\n            lines.append(\"-\" * 30)\n            lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"#{task['id']} — {task['name']}\")\n        lines.append(f\"    Status: {task['status']}\")\n        lines.append(f\"    Next checkpoint: {format_date(next_checkpoint(task))}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    lines.append(\"Reply with the IDs of your top 3 priorities for this week.\")\n\n    message = MIMEText(\"\\n\".join(lines))\n    message[\"Subject\"] = subject\n    message[\"From\"] = settings[\"from_address\"]\n    message[\"To\"] = settings[\"to_address\"]\n    message[\"Message-ID\"] = make_msgid()\n    return message\n\n\ndef send_review_email(tasks: list[dict], settings: dict) -> None:\n    if not tasks:\n        log(\"No open tasks — skipping weekly review email.\")\n        return\n\n    message = build_review_email(tasks, settings)\n    smtp_host = settings[\"smtp_host\"]\n    smtp_port = settings[\"smtp_port\"]\n    try:\n        with smtplib.SMTP(smtp_host, smtp_port, timeout=5) as server:\n            server.send_message(message)\n    except OSError as error:\n        log(f\"Could not send weekly review email via {smtp_host}:{smtp_port}: {error}\")\n        return\n\n    log(f\"Sent weekly review email ({len(tasks)} task(s)), subject: {message['Subject']}\")"
}

  "new_string": "    while True:\n        tasks = fetch_open_tasks()\n        if tasks is not None:\n            names = [task[\"name\"] for task in tasks]\n            log(f\"{len(tasks)} open task(s): {names}\")\n\n            now = time.monotonic()\n            if last_review_sent is None or now - last_review_sent >= REVIEW_EMAIL_INTERVAL_SECONDS:\n                send_review_email(tasks, fetch_settings())\n                last_review_sent = now\n\n        check_for_replies()\n        time.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS)"

Title: Settings | Nudge

Source element: <main>
---
Settings
Email

Used by the notification-worker to send the weekly review email (SMTP only — never used to read mail).

SMTP HOST
SMTP PORT
FROM ADDRESS
TO ADDRESS
Checkpoints

Default checkpoint spacing for new tasks, as a percentage of the buffer window (creation → buffer deadline). Existing tasks keep the values they were created with.

CHECKPOINT 1 (%)
CHECKPOINT 2 (%)
Escalation

When a task counts as "urgent" (All-Tasks grouping), and who gets notified if it stays unresolved.

URGENT THRESHOLD (DAYS BEFORE BUFFER DEADLINE)
BUDDY NAME
BUDDY EMAIL
Display

How dates and times are shown throughout the app.

DATE FORMAT
Jul 12, 2026 11:08 AM
12 Jul 2026, 11:08
07/12/2026 11:08 AM
12/07/2026 11:08
2026-07-12 11:08
Save Settings

Claude12.07.2026 19:36

Date format applied globally. Let's create a new task to verify the checkpoint ratio setting (40%) actually applies.

Claude12.07.2026 19:36
Bash
{
  "command": "curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/tasks -d \"name=Settings Ratio Test&external_deadline=2026-08-01T12:00\" -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\\n\"\ncurl -s http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/tasks | python3 -c \"\nimport json, sys\nfrom datetime import datetime\ntasks = json.load(sys.stdin)\nt = next(x for x in tasks if x['name'] == 'Settings Ratio Test')\ncreated = datetime.fromisoformat(t['created_at'])\nbuffer = datetime.fromisoformat(t['buffer_deadline'])\ncp1 = datetime.fromisoformat(t['checkpoint_1'])\ncp2 = datetime.fromisoformat(t['checkpoint_2'])\nwindow = (buffer - created).total_seconds()\nratio1 = (cp1 - created).total_seconds() / window\nratio2 = (cp2 - created).total_seconds() / window\nprint(f'checkpoint_1 ratio: {ratio1:.3f} (expected 0.40)')\nprint(f'checkpoint_2 ratio: {ratio2:.3f} (expected 0.75)')\n\"",
  "description": "Create a task and verify it uses the updated checkpoint 1 ratio (40%)"
}

… [truncated, 3669 more chars]
Claude12.07.2026 19:45

Found it — that was my mistake, not a bug in the app. send_review_email() only ever runs *inside* the if tasks is not None: block, so when core-app is fully down, the worker correctly skips sending the review email entirely (it has no task data to put in it) — which means fetch_settings() never even gets called in this scenario, so the log line I was waiting for structurally can't appear. That matches the same resilience pattern proven back in Step 6, not a bug.

Let me restart core-app and confirm normal operation resumes.

Claude12.07.2026 19:45
Bash

  "command": "cd /d/xampp/htdocs/nudge/core-app\n./.venv/Scripts/python.exe app.py > /tmp/settings_coreapp2.log 2>&1 &\nsleep 2\ntail -3 /tmp/settings_coreapp2.log",
  "description": "Restart core-app"
}

Confirmed working correctly — worker recovered automatically and resumed sending with the settings-driven addresses. Let's clean everything up and finalize this step.

Claude12.07.2026 19:47
PowerShell
{
  "command": "$procs = Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter \"Name='python.exe'\" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like '*core-app*' -or $_.CommandLine -like '*notification-worker*' }\n$mailpitProcs = Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter \"Name='mailpit.exe'\"\nforeach ($p in $procs) { Stop-Process -Id $p.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }\nforeach ($p in $mailpitProcs) { Stop-Process -Id $p.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }\nStart-Sleep -Seconds 1\n\"stopped $($procs.Count) app/worker and $($mailpitProcs.Count) mailpit processes\"",
  "description": "Stop all test processes"
}

Now let's write the step documentation and update the backlog/checklist.

Claude12.07.2026 19:48
Write
{
  "file_path": "D:\\xampp\\htdocs\\nudge\\docs\\14-step10b-settings.md",
  "content": "# Step 10b: Settings-Screen\n\n**Status:** Abgeschlossen\n**Bezug:** ESC-FUNC-003 (Urgent-Schwellenwert konfigurierbar), Vorarbeit für ESC-FUNC-004/005 (Buddy-Kontakt-Eingabe, Logik folgt in Schritt 10c). Aus dem Backlog: Settings-Screen, konfigurierbares Datum-/Zeitformat.\n\n---\n\n## Architekturentscheidung: Settings leben in core-app, der Worker pollt sie\n\n`notification-worker` hat keine eigene Datenbank und keine UI — beides existiert ausschließlich in `core-app`. Ein Settings-*Screen* kann also nur dort entstehen. Für die Werte, die eigentlich das Verhalten des Workers steuern (SMTP-Host/Port, Absender-/Empfängeradresse), gilt deshalb dasselbe Muster wie schon für Tasks: `core-app` ist die alleinige Quelle der Wahrheit, der Worker fragt sie per REST ab (`GET /api/settings`, neu, analog zu `GET /api/tasks`). Kein neues Architekturmuster — nur eine Erweiterung des bereits bestehenden Polling-Ansatzes auf eine zweite Ressource.\n\n## Was gebaut wurde\n\n### `settings`-Tabelle (Singleton-Row)\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS settings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),\n    smtp_host TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'localhost',\n    smtp_port INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1025,\n    from_address TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'nudge@localhost',\n    to_address TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user@localhost',\n    checkpoint_1_ratio REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.5,\n    checkpoint_2_ratio REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.75,\n    urgent_threshold_days REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 3,\n    buddy_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n    buddy_email TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n    date_format TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p'\n)\n```\n\n`CHECK (id = 1)` erzwingt auf Schemaebene, dass es exakt eine Zeile geben kann — kein generischer Key-Value-Store (wäre für eine feste, bekannte Anzahl an Einstellungen unnötig generisch und in der Prüfung schwerer zu erklären als benannte Spalten). `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO settings (id) VALUES (1)` bei jedem `init_db()`-Aufruf legt die Default-Zeile beim ersten Start an und ist bei jedem weiteren Start ein No-op.\n\n### Berechnungsfunktionen nehmen jetzt Parameter statt globaler Konstanten\n\n`calculate_buffer_deadline`, `calculate_checkpoints` und `is_urgent` hatten die Verhältnis-/Schwellenwerte bisher als Modul-Konstanten fest verdrahtet. Jetzt akzeptieren sie optionale Parameter (Default = bisherige Konstante, für Abwärtskompatibilität und Tests ohne Settings-Kontext):\n\n```python\ndef calculate_checkpoints(created_at, buffer_deadline,\n                           checkpoint_1_ratio=CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO,\n                           checkpoint_2_ratio=CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO):\n    ...\n\ndef is_urgent(task, threshold_days=URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS):\n    ...\n```\n\n`create_task()` liest die aktuellen Settings einmal selbst (`get_settings()`) und reicht die Checkpoint-Verhältnisse durch — Task-Anlage ist ein seltener, nicht performance-kritischer Vorgang, ein zusätzlicher DB-Read fällt nicht ins Gewicht. **Wichtig:** Bereits angelegte Tasks behalten ihre zum Anlagezeitpunkt berechneten Checkpoint-Daten — eine spätere Settings-Änderung wirkt sich nur auf künftige Tasks aus, nicht rückwirkend. `BUFFER_RATIO` (0,7) bleibt bewusst **kein** Setting — weder im Backlog-Eintrag noch in der Anforderungsliste war das gefordert, nur die Checkpoint-Intervalle.\n\n### `friendly_date` und `g.settings`: einmal pro Request, nicht einmal pro Datum\n\nEine Seite wie All-Tasks rendert leicht ein Dutzend Datumsangaben. Statt bei jedem `|friendly_date`-Filteraufruf erneut die Datenbank zu lesen, lädt ein `@app.before_request`-Hook die Settings einmal pro Anfrage in Flasks anfrage-gebundenes `g`-Objekt:\n\n```python\n@app.before_request\ndef load_settings():\n    g.settings = get_settings()\n\ndef friendly_date(value):\n    if not value:\n        return \"\"\n    date_format = g.settings[\"date_format\"] if \"settings\" in g else \"%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p\"\n    return datetime.fromisoformat(value).strftime(date_format)\n```\n\n`is_urgent()`-Aufrufe in der All-Tasks-Route bekommen den aktuellen Schwellenwert ebenfalls aus `g.settings` übergeben, statt ihn selbst nachzuschlagen — dieselbe Überlegung (die Route ruft `is_urgent()` einmal pro Task auf, der Schwellenwert soll nur einmal gelesen werden).\n\n### Settings-Formular (`/settings`)\n\nVier Abschnitte, angelehnt an die im Screen-Audit (`02c`) als grundsätzlich übernehmbar eingestuften Teile von `nudge_settings`, um zwei neue erweitert:\n- **Email** — SMTP-Host/-Port, Absender-/Empfängeradresse (steuert den Worker).\n- **Checkpoints** — Checkpoint-1/-2-Prozentsätze für neue Tasks (Formular zeigt Prozent, `update_settings()` rechnet in Verhältniswerte 0–1 um).\n- **Escalation** — Urgent-Schwellenwert in Tagen (ESC-FUNC-003), Buddy-Name/-Email (Eingabefeld für Schritt 10c vorbereitet, noch ohne Eskalationslogik dahinter).\n- **Display** — Datumsformat, fünf feste Presets (US mit Monatsname, EU mit Monatsname, US numerisch, EU numerisch, ISO) statt eines Freitext-`strftime`-Feldes — weniger fehleranfällig für eine UI-Auswahl, einfacher zu erklären als ein rohes Format-Pattern.\n\n`POST /settings` übernimmt nur eine feste Menge benannter Felder aus `request.form` (kein `**request.form` direkt an `update_settings()` durchgereicht) — verhindert, dass ein manipulierter Formular-Feldname zu einer unbeabsichtigten Spalten-Injektion führen könnte, auch wenn das Risiko bei einer Solo-App mit einer einzigen Nutzerin gering ist.\n\n### Worker: `fetch_settings()` mit Fallback\n\n```python\nDEFAULT_SETTINGS = {\n    \"smtp_host\": \"localhost\", \"smtp_port\": 1025,\n    \"from_address\": \"nudge@localhost\", \"to_address\": \"user@localhost\",\n}\n\ndef fetch_settings() -> dict:\n    url = f\"{CORE_APP_URL}/api/settings\"\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=5) as response:\n            return json.loads(response.read())\n    except urllib.error.URLError as error:\n        log(f\"Could not fetch settings from core-app at {url}, using defaults: {error}\")\n        return DEFAULT_SETTINGS\n```\n\nWird bei jedem Versand-Zyklus neu aufgerufen (nicht einmalig beim Start gecacht) — eine Settings-Änderung wirkt sich damit spätestens beim nächsten Versand aus, ohne den Worker neu starten zu müssen. Gleiches Resilienz-Muster wie `fetch_open_tasks()`: nicht erreichbares core-app führt zu geloggten Fallback-Defaults, nicht zum Absturz. POP3-Zugangsdaten (`POP3_USERNAME`/`PASSWORD`) bleiben bewusst weiterhin Code-Konstanten — nicht Teil des ursprünglichen Backlog-Eintrags (\"SMTP-Konfiguration\"), keine ungefragte Scope-Erweiterung.\n\n## Verifikation\n\n1. `/api/settings` direkt nach Erststart geprüft → korrekte Default-Werte, Singleton-Zeile existiert.\n2. `/settings`-Formular geöffnet, alle vier Abschnitte korrekt vorbefüllt, keine Konsolenfehler.\n3. Werte geändert (Datumsformat → ISO, Checkpoint 1 → 40 %, Urgent-Schwellenwert → 5 Tage, Buddy-Kontakt gesetzt) und gespeichert → `GET /api/settings` bestätigt alle neuen Werte, Formular zeigt nach Reload dieselben Werte (Redirect-Then-Reload-Zyklus funktioniert).\n4. Datumsformat-Änderung wirkt sich sofort auf `/tasks` aus — alle angezeigten Daten wechseln von `Jul 12, 2026 11:08 AM` zu `2026-07-12 11:58`, ohne Serverneustart.\n5. Neuer Task angelegt, Checkpoint-1-Verhältnis rechnerisch aus den gespeicherten Zeitstempeln zurückgerechnet → exakt 0,40, wie in Settings hinterlegt. Checkpoint 2 (unverändert 75 %) weiterhin korrekt.\n6. Mailpit + Worker gestartet, Settings zuvor auf klar erkennbare Test-Adressen geändert (`settings-test@nudge.local` / `settings-recipient@nudge.local`) → verschickte Review-Mail in Mailpit trägt exakt diese Absender-/Empfängeradresse, nicht die alten Code-Konstanten.\n7. core-app während laufendem Worker beendet → Worker überspringt den Versand korrekt (kein Task-Datensatz zum Versenden vorhanden, gleiches Verhalten wie in Schritt 6 etabliert), kein Absturz. core-app neu gestartet → Worker verbindet sich automatisch wieder und versendet beim nächsten fälligen Zyklus mit den weiterhin korrekten (Settings-gespeicherten) Adressen.\n\nTestdatensatz danach neu geseedet (`seed_data.py`), Settings dadurch wieder auf Defaults zurückgesetzt.\n\n## Nächster Schritt\n\nSchritt 10c: Buddy-System (ESC-FUNC-004, 005) — Eskalationsstufe 3 nutzt den in diesem Schritt bereits eingerichteten Buddy-Kontakt aus den Settings.\n"
}

42	- [x] **ESC-FUNC-001** (Must) — Jeder Task hat einen berechneten (nicht manuell gesetzten) Zustand "Urgent" ja/nein, basierend auf Restzeit bis Buffer-Deadline (Default-Schwellenwert: < 3 Tage). *(Schritt 10a — `is_urgent()`, reine Funktion, kein gespeichertes Feld. Kein "Important"-Feld eingeführt, siehe `13-step10a-dashboard-and-eisenhower.md`.)*
43	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-002** (Must) — Nur Tasks im Zustand "Important + Urgent" lösen die volle Eskalationskette (Reminder, ggf. Buddy-Alert) aus. "Important + Not Urgent"-Tasks werden getrackt, aber ohne aktive Benachrichtigung.
44	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-003** (Should) — Der Urgent-Schwellenwert ist in den Settings konfigurierbar (Default-Wert reicht für MVP-Funktionalität).
45	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-004** (Should) — Ein hinterlegter Accountability-Buddy-Kontakt (Settings) wird bei Eskalationsstufe 3 (75%-Checkpoint verpasst / Buffer-Deadline überschritten) für "Important + Urgent"-Tasks automatisch benachrichtigt.
46	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-005** (Should) — Die Buddy-Benachrichtigung enthält nur "Task X braucht Aufmerksamkeit", keine vollen Task-Details.
47	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-006** (Could) — Pro-Task individualisierbare Urgent-Schwellenwerte.
48	- [ ] **ESC-FUNC-007** (Could) — Live-Neuberechnung des Eisenhower-Quadranten bei jedem App-Öffnen statt nur im täglichen Cron-Durchlauf.

1	# Backlog: Bewusst zurückgestellte Ideen
2	
3	Sammelstelle für Punkte, die während des Baus aufkamen, aber bewusst nicht in den MVP-Scope gezogen wurden — kein Vollständigkeits-Versäumnis, sondern dokumentierte Entscheidung (siehe `03-mvp-requirements-checklist.md`, Abschnitt "Bewusst nicht atomarisiert" für dasselbe Prinzip). Jeder Eintrag verlinkt zurück zu der Stelle, an der er aufkam, für den vollen Kontext. Lebendes Dokument — bei Bedarf ergänzen, bei Umsetzung hierher als "erledigt" markieren oder entfernen.
4	
5	---
6	
7	- [ ] **HTML-formatierte Review-Mail** statt reinem Plaintext (`notification-worker/worker.py`, `build_review_email`). Aufgeworfen nach Schritt 6. Würde z. B. echte visuelle Blöcke statt Trennlinien-Zeichen, anklickbare Task-Links zurück in die Kern-App etc. ermöglichen. Reiner Plaintext war für den MVP bewusst einfacher (kein HTML-Templating im Worker nötig, in jedem Mail-Client lesbar, leicht in der Prüfung zu erklären).
8	
9	- [ ] **14-Tage-Fenster + Cap bei 10 dringendsten Tasks** für die Review-Mail, falls sich in der Praxis zeigt, dass die reine Block-Formatierung (Schritt 6) bei sehr vielen offenen Tasks nicht ausreicht. Siehe `09-step6-email-and-mailpit.md`, Abschnitt "Nachtrag aus der Nutzerinnen-Review" für die volle Abwägung gegen REVIEW-FUNC-003.
10	
11	- [ ] **Deadline-lose Tasks** (Backlog-/Ideen-Objekttyp, nimmt nicht am Checkpoint-/Buffer-/Eisenhower-Mechanismus teil). Erste Designidee: automatischer Wiedervorlage-Reminder ca. einen Monat nach Anlage. Siehe `05-step2-task-model.md`, Abschnitt "Scope-Frage während Review".
12	
13	- [ ] **KI-gestütztes Reply-Parsing** (lokales Modell via Ollama) als dritter, unabhängiger Prozess, ergänzend zum regelbasierten Parsing aus Schritt 7 — nicht als Ersatz. Siehe `00-kickoff-briefing.md`, Abschnitt 7.
14	
15	- [ ] **Settings-Screen** (SMTP-Konfiguration fürs UI statt hartkodierter Konstanten in `worker.py`, Checkpoint-Intervall-Overrides, Urgent-Schwellenwert, Accountability-Buddy-Kon

{
  "command": "cd /d/xampp/htdocs/nudge\ngit commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nAdd quick-add FAB to All-Tasks, fix list-view spacing, polish triage/timer nav\n\nAll-Tasks gets a small floating \"+\" button opening a modal with the\nsame add-task form used on the Dashboard (extracted into a shared\n_add_task_form.html macro, imported by both pages, instead of\nduplicating the markup) -- deliberately less prominent than the\nDashboard's inline form since All-Tasks is meant to be the backlog\nview, not the primary add-task flow.\n\nFixes a real bug found in review: the List-view container had\nflex-col and gap-3 but was missing the base flex display class, so\nTailwind's gap utility never actually applied (gap only works inside\nflex/grid containers) -- task rows were rendering with no spacing\nbetween them.\n\nAlso two navigation fixes on the triage/timer flow found by manually\nwalking through it: the three blocker-reason buttons now get a\nvisible accent border when selected (data-reason attribute + class\ntoggle), and the timer page's back link now returns to the triage\nselection screen instead of jumping straight to the task detail page.\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Commit UI polish changes"
}

{
  "command": "cd /d/xampp/htdocs/nudge\ngit commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nAdd Settings screen (ESC-FUNC-003), core-app as the single source of config\n\nNew settings table (singleton row, CHECK id = 1) holding SMTP config,\ncheckpoint-1/2 ratio defaults, urgent threshold (ESC-FUNC-003), a\nbuddy contact (input ready for Step 10c's escalation logic), and a\ndate/time format. core-app is the only process with a UI, so it's\nalso the only source of truth -- the worker fetches its own operating\nconfig from a new GET /api/settings each send cycle rather than using\nhardcoded constants, same polling pattern already used for tasks and\nfocus-task writeback. Falls back to sane defaults and logs if\ncore-app is unreachable, same resilience pattern as fetch_open_tasks().\n\ncalculate_buffer_deadline/calculate_checkpoints/is_urgent now take\nthe relevant ratios/threshold as parameters (defaulting to the old\nmodule constants) instead of reading them as closed-over globals --\ncreate_task() and the All-Tasks route pass the current settings in\nexplicitly. Settings are loaded once per request into Flask's g\nobject so friendly_date() doesn't hit the database for every date on\na page.\n\nCloses out two backlog items (Settings screen, configurable date\nformat) and ESC-FUNC-003 in the requirements checklist.\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1\ngit push 2>&1",
  "description": "Commit Settings feature and push both commits"
}

0
Claude12.07.2026 19:50

Both commits pushed. Summary of everything from this stretch:

UI polish (9526cfb):


  • Quick-add FAB on All-Tasks reusing the Dashboard's form via a shared _add_task_form.html macro

  • Fixed the List-view spacing bug (missing flex class meant gap-3 never applied)

  • Triage reason buttons now show a visible border when selected; Timer's back link goes to Triage instead of the task detail page

Settings screen (4e0e0a4), closing ESC-FUNC-003 plus two backlog items:


  • New settings table in core-app (SMTP config, checkpoint ratios, urgent threshold, buddy contact — ready for 10c, date format)

  • Worker now polls GET /api/settings each cycle instead of using hardcoded constants, same pattern as task polling — falls back to defaults and logs if core-app's unreachable

  • friendly_date() reads the configured format via a per-request g.settings cache so it's not hitting the DB for every date on a page

I tested this pretty thoroughly, including one dead-end worth mentioning: I spent a while waiting for a "settings fallback" log line that structurally couldn't appear, because send_review_email() only runs when the task fetch already succeeded — so when core-app is fully down, the worker correctly skips sending rather than trying to email with no data. Good behavior, just a flawed test on my part, not a bug.

Everything's cleaned up and the test DB is back to defaults. Ready for Step 10c: the buddy system (ESC-FUNC-004/005) whenever you want to continue.

Du12.07.2026 19:58

time and date format picker is a bit misleading. checkpoint % are hardcoded in single-task-view (I changed it to 40% for testing). Since it says old tasks aren't adjusted, maybe a short (e.g. 50%) or (default: 50%) would clear this up.

we also need a timezone setting and I'm thinking maybe we should round up on the checkin/buffer-deadline times etc.

for example I created "Test Percentages" (40%) and Test Percentages II (back to 50%) and they both have a deadline on 05:22 AM which is just an unusual number tbh

Claude12.07.2026 19:59

Both the format-preview and checkpoint-label issues are real bugs — let me fix those now. The timezone and rounding requests are genuinely new scope with real design forks, so let me ask about those before touching anything.

Claude12.07.2026 19:59
AskUserQuestion
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "question": "Timezone support means every stored timestamp needs to become UTC internally, converted to your configured zone only at display time (Python's stdlib zoneinfo, no new dependency) — touches task creation, checkpoint math, and every date display. Given the assignment deadline pressure we've talked about, build it now or backlog it? Right now the app just uses your machine's local time everywhere, which is correct as long as you're the only one running it on your own machine.",
      "header": "Timezone scope",
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Backlog it (Recommended)",
          "description": "Not currently causing a real problem — server and viewer are the same machine/timezone. Worth doing properly later (e.g. before any real deployment), not as a quick add now."
        },
        {
          "label": "Build it now",
          "description": "Bigger piece of work: UTC storage, a timezone Settings field (IANA name like 'Europe/Berlin'), and updating every place 'now' is computed or a date is shown."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "question": "For rounding computed checkpoint/buffer-deadline times (avoiding results like 5:22 AM) — which direction and granularity?",
      "header": "Rounding rule",
      "options": [
        {
          "label": "Round down to the hour (Recommended)",
          "description": "5:22 AM → 5:00 AM. Matches the 'buffer by default, safe option wins' principle already used for the 0.7 buffer ratio — never gives you less margin than calculated, only ever a bit more (checkpoint/deadline effectively a little earlier)."
        },
        {
          "label": "Round up to the hour",
          "description": "5:22 AM → 6:00 AM, as you described. Cleaner-sounding but shaves a little margin off every checkpoint and the buffer deadline itself."
        },
        {
          "label": "Round to nearest 15 minutes",
          "description": "5:22 AM → 5:15 AM. Finer-grained than whole hours, still clears up the odd-looking minute values."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Right now the app just uses your machine's local time everywhere, which is correct as long as you're the only one running it on your own machine."="Backlog it (Recommended)", "For rounding computed checkpoint/buffer-deadline times (avoiding results like 5:22 AM) — which direction and granularity?"="round to nearest 30 min". 1	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
2	
3	from db import get_connection
4	
5	BUFFER_RATIO = 0.7
6	CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO = 0.5
7	CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO = 0.75
8	URGENT_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 3
9	
10	
11	def calculate_buffer_deadline(
12	    created_at: datetime, external_deadline: datetime, buffer_ratio: float = BUFFER_RATIO
13	) -> datetime:
14	    available_time = external_deadline - created_at
15	    return created_at + buffer_ratio * available_time
16	
17	
18	def calculate_checkpoints(
19	    created_at: datetime,
20	    buffer_deadline: datetime,
21	    checkpoint_1_ratio: float = CHECKPOINT_1_RATIO,
22	    checkpoint_2_ratio: float = CHECKPOINT_2_RATIO,
23	) -> tuple[datetime, datetime]:
24	    buffer_window = buffer_deadline - created_at
25	    checkpoint_1 = created_at + checkpoint_1_ratio * buffer_window
26	    checkpoint_2 = created_at + checkpoint_2_ratio * buffer_window
27	    return checkpoint_1, checkpoint_2
